description: Send 3 distinct messages in succession; each must echo back and persist exactly once per direction (stresses the cross-path dedup under multiple sends — the area the original double-write bug lived; also the standing regression for the retracted msgID-collision class). Requires echo_live. #18 (codex-vetted 2026-05-30): texts carry a per-run {{nonce}}, so the earlier 4/8 failure mode (a FIXED text like multi-a1 accumulating across back-to-back runs when a straggler echo lands after the bounded reset → `count=4`) can no longer inflate the assertions. PROMOTED to a HARD GATE 2026-05-30: measured 33/33 back-to-back PASS on the nonce build (8 + 25) plus 3 clean full-suite runs, meeting codex's 20-30-run bar, so nonBlocking was removed.
